scenarios.title: Incumbent displacement by new entrants
scenarios.description: New entrants with lower cost structures gain market share as incumbents struggle to adapt. The causal chain: cost advantage → price undercut → margin compression → incumbent investment cuts → further competitive gap.

scenarios.title: Sector consolidation via M&A wave
scenarios.description: Rising interest rates and margin compression force smaller players to merge with or be acquired by larger competitors. Three to five major players remain.

scenarios.title: Market fragmentation via specialization
scenarios.description: Demand heterogeneity drives niche specialists who outcompete generalists in each segment. The market fragments into vertically-specialized players rather than consolidating.

scenarios.title: Regulatory inversion reverses competitive dynamics
scenarios.description: A major regulatory shift fundamentally changes who can compete and on what terms. Current market leaders become disadvantaged; previously marginal players gain structural advantages.
key_assumptions: Regulatory change is large and fast
